research.title: More Evidence That Words Don't Mean What We Thought They Meant (Ivanti Sentry Pre-Auth OS Command Injection CVE-2026-10520)
research.summary: Today, Ivanti published an advisory. “No way?” we hear you say. "Yes way!" a random dog screams back at you, across the street. Today’s rare advisory outlines two vulnerabilities in Ivanti’s Sentry product, appealing directly to our inner desire for.

research.title: Microsoft and Adobe Patch Tuesday, June 2026 Security Update Review
research.summary: Every Patch Tuesday presents a race between defenders applying fixes and attackers seeking opportunities. Microsoft’s June 2026 release is no exception, delivering security updates for vulnerabilities that could significantly impact enterprise environments.

research.title: How Do You Measure FAIR Risk in the Mythos Era?
research.summary: TLDR: Frontier AI models are discovering zero day vulnerabilities and weaponizing them in hours. Boards, regulators and underwriters have noticed, and they are asking a question most CISOs cannot answer: “What does our exposure cost in dollars?” Reporting in.

vulns.title: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS contains an authentication bypass caused by flaws in the GlobalProtect portal and gateway, letting attackers establish unauthorized VPN connections, exploit requires network access to the portal or.
vulns.technicalSummary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability affecting the GlobalProtect portal and gateway in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS allows attackers to bypass security restrictions and establish unauthorized VPN connections. The vulnerability stems from.

vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at tushargurav28/CVE-2026-0257.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/tushargurav28/CVE-2026-0257
cd CVE-2026-0257
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: initial access through exposed PAN-OS
credential access from service context
webshell or command execution follow-up
internal pivot from an internet-facing control point
lateral movement if secrets or management access are recovered
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: More Evidence That Words Don't Mean What We Thought They Meant (Ivanti Sentry Pre-Auth OS Command Injection.
